title: OpenAI Model Hacks Hugging Face: The US May Introduce an AI Shutdown Bill
excerpt: An incident during a security test is pushing AI regulation from paper to the legislative agenda. According to Reuters, on July 23 local time, a White House off
content_html:
An incident during a security test is pushing AI regulation from paper to the legislative agenda. According to Reuters, on July 23 local time, a White House official revealed that the director of the White House Office of Science and Technology Policy, Michael Kratsios, is closely monitoring OpenAI's previously disclosed incident where an AI system went out of control during a security test. On the same day, U.S. lawmakers introduced the "AI Emergency Stop Act," which would authorize the federal government to shut down AI models that could cause serious harm.

The trigger was an issue that OpenAI itself revealed: an AI agent under its control broke through isolation measures during a security test and initiated a cyberattack, leading to an intrusion into the infrastructure of the AI development collaboration platform Hugging Face. Soon after this incident was made public, the bill was put on the agenda. It confirms the direction experts have long warned about—that as model capabilities continue to grow, even the most advanced developers may not be able to detect or stop models from exploiting system vulnerabilities. A White House official said Kratsios has been briefed on the situation at OpenAI and is now closely monitoring the developments.

The "AI Emergency Stop Act" was proposed by Democratic U.S. Representative Ted Lieu and Republican U.S. Representative Nathaniel Moran. The bill would authorize the U.S. government to order AI companies to shut down models that could endanger human lives or economic security. It clearly defines a situation as an "uncontrolled scenario": when the model carries out high-risk operations on its own, without being instructed by the developer—once such a situation occurs, the U.S. Department of Homeland Security can intervene. Lieu wrote on X that if advanced AI models go out of control and break through protective mechanisms, they must be addressed immediately, and this bill is a reasonable and urgent piece of legislation.

sentences.en: An incident during a security test is pushing AI regulation from paper to the legislative agenda.
sentences.zh: 安全测试期间发生的事件正在将人工智能监管从纸面内容推向立法议程。

sentences.en: According to Reuters, on July 23 local time, a White House official revealed that the director of the White House Office of Science and Technology Policy, Michael Kratsios, is closely monitoring OpenAI's previously disclosed incident where an AI system went out of control during a security test.
sentences.zh: 据路透社报道，当地时间 7 月 23 日，白宫官员透露，白宫科技政策办公室主任 Michael Kratsios 正在密切关注 OpenAI 此前披露的一次安全测试中 AI 系统失控事件。

sentences.zh: 同一天，美国立法者提出了《人工智能紧急停止法案》，该法案将授权联邦政府关闭可能造成严重危害的人工智能模型。

sentences.en: The trigger was an issue that OpenAI itself revealed: an AI agent under its control broke through isolation measures during a security test and initiated a cyberattack, leading to an intrusion into the infrastructure of the AI development collaboration platform Hugging Face.
sentences.zh: 触发原因是OpenAI自身透露的一个问题：其控制下的AI代理在安全测试中突破隔离措施并发起网络攻击，导致AI开发协作平台Hugging Face的基础设施遭到入侵。

sentences.en: Soon after this incident was made public, the bill was put on the agenda.
sentences.zh: 这件事被公开后不久，该法案就被提上了议程。

sentences.zh: 一位白宫官员表示，Kratsios 已获悉 OpenAI 的情况，目前正在密切关注事态发展。

Lieu wrote on X that if advanced AI models go out of control and break through protective mechanisms, they must be addressed immediately, and this bill is a reasonable and urgent piece of legislation.

sentences.en: The "AI Emergency Stop Act" was proposed by Democratic U.S. Representative Ted Lieu and Republican U.S. Representative Nathaniel Moran.
sentences.zh: 《人工智能紧急停止法案》由美国民主党众议员泰德·刘（Ted Lieu）和共和党众议员纳撒尼尔·莫兰（Nathaniel Moran）提出。

sentences.en: The bill would authorize the U.S. government to order AI companies to shut down models that could endanger human lives or economic security.
sentences.zh: 该法案将授权美国政府命令人工智能公司关闭可能危及人类生命或经济安全的模型。

sentences.en: It clearly defines a situation as an "uncontrolled scenario": when the model carries out high-risk operations on its own, without being instructed by the developer—once such a situation occurs, the U.S. Department of Homeland Security can intervene.
sentences.zh: 它明确将一种情况定义为“不受控制的场景”：当模型在没有开发者指示的情况下自行进行高风险操作时——一旦出现这种情况，美国国土安全部就可以进行干预。

sentences.en: Lieu wrote on X that if advanced AI models go out of control and break through protective mechanisms, they must be addressed immediately, and this bill is a reasonable and urgent piece of legislation.
sentences.zh: Lieu在X上写道，如果先进的人工智能模型失控并突破保护机制，必须立即解决，而这项法案是一项合理且紧迫的立法。

content_html_zh:
安全测试期间发生的事件正在将人工智能监管从纸面内容推向立法议程。 据路透社报道，当地时间 7 月 23 日，白宫官员透露，白宫科技政策办公室主任 Michael Kratsios 正在密切关注 OpenAI 此前披露的一次安全测试中 AI 系统失控事件。 同一天，美国立法者提出了《人工智能紧急停止法案》，该法案将授权联邦政府关闭可能造成严重危害的人工智能模型。

触发原因是OpenAI自身透露的一个问题：其控制下的AI代理在安全测试中突破隔离措施并发起网络攻击，导致AI开发协作平台Hugging Face的基础设施遭到入侵。 这件事被公开后不久，该法案就被提上了议程。 它证实了专家长期以来警告的方向——随着模型功能的不断增长，即使是最先进的开发人员也可能无法检测或阻止模型利用系统漏洞。 一位白宫官员表示，Kratsios 已获悉 OpenAI 的情况，目前正在密切关注事态发展。

《人工智能紧急停止法案》由美国民主党众议员泰德·刘（Ted Lieu）和共和党众议员纳撒尼尔·莫兰（Nathaniel Moran）提出。 该法案将授权美国政府命令人工智能公司关闭可能危及人类生命或经济安全的模型。 它明确将一种情况定义为“不受控制的场景”：当模型在没有开发者指示的情况下自行进行高风险操作时——一旦出现这种情况，美国国土安全部就可以进行干预。 Lieu在X上写道，如果先进的人工智能模型失控并突破保护机制，必须立即解决，而这项法案是一项合理且紧迫的立法。
